root_text.an5.161:1.1: “Pañcime, bhikkhave, āghātapaṭivinayā yattha bhikkhuno uppanno āghāto sabbaso paṭivinetabbo.
root_text.an5.161:1.3: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, puggale āghāto jāyetha, mettā tasmiṁ puggale bhāvetabbā;
root_text.an5.161:1.5: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, puggale āghāto jāyetha, karuṇā tasmiṁ puggale bhāvetabbā;
root_text.an5.161:1.7: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, puggale āghāto jāyetha, upekkhā tasmiṁ puggale bhāvetabbā;
root_text.an5.161:1.9: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, puggale āghāto jāyetha, asatiamanasikāro tasmiṁ puggale āpajjitabbo;
root_text.an5.161:1.11: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, puggale āghāto jāyetha, kammassakatā tasmiṁ puggale adhiṭṭhātabbā:
root_text.an5.161:1.13: yaṁ kammaṁ karissati kalyāṇaṁ vā pāpakaṁ vā tassa dāyādo bhavissatī’ti;
root_text.an5.161:1.15: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca āghātapaṭivinayā, yattha bhikkhuno uppanno āghāto sabbaso paṭivinetabbo”ti.

translation_text.an5.161:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant should use these five ways to completely get rid of resentment when it has arisen toward anyone.
translation_text.an5.161:1.3: You should develop love for an individual you resent.
translation_text.an5.161:1.4: That’s how to get rid of resentment for that individual.
translation_text.an5.161:1.5: You should develop compassion for an individual you resent. …
translation_text.an5.161:1.7: You should develop equanimity for an individual you resent. …
translation_text.an5.161:1.9: You should disregard an individual you resent, paying no attention to them. …
translation_text.an5.161:1.11: You should focus on the ownership of deeds regarding that individual:
translation_text.an5.161:1.12: ‘This venerable is the owner of their deeds and heir to their deeds. Deeds are their womb, their relative, and their refuge.
translation_text.an5.161:1.13: They shall be the heir of whatever deeds they do, whether good or bad.’
translation_text.an5.161:1.14: That’s how to get rid of resentment for that individual.
translation_text.an5.161:1.15: A mendicant should use these five ways to completely get rid of resentment when it has arisen toward anyone.”
